name: Asparagus and Beef with Black Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Asparagus; cut into 1/2-in thick diagonal pieces
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------BEEF MARINADE-----------------------
1. In a bowl, combine 1/2 tsp sugar, 1 tbsp rice wine or dry sherry, 1/2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp cornstarch, and 1/2 tsp sesame oil to make the beef marinade.
2. Cover the black beans with warm water for 5 minutes. Drain and rinse thoroughly to remove salt. Mash the black beans with garlic and ginger into a paste and set aside.
3. Slice the flank steak against the grain into 1/4-inch thick slices. Toss with the beef marinade and let sit for 10 minutes.
4. Preheat a wok until hot. Add 2 tbsp peanut oil.
5. Over high heat, add the beef and stir-fry for a minute or until seared. Remove and set aside.
6. In the same wok, add the remaining oil, salt, and the black bean paste. Stir-fry until fragrant, about a few seconds.
7. Add the onions and stir-fry for 10 seconds. Then add the asparagus in 2 or 3 batches, making sure the wok is hot before adding the next batch. Toss to coat with oil.
8. Sprinkle with dark soy sauce and toss together.
9. Pour in the chicken stock and bring to a boil. If additional cooking is needed for the asparagus, cover for about 1 minute.
10. In a small bowl, mix 2 tsp water, 1/2 tsp sugar, 1/2 tbsp oyster sauce, and a dash of sesame oil until smooth.
11. Once the asparagus is tender but still crisp, add the cornstarch mixture to the center of the wok.
12. Stir for about 10 seconds until thickened. Return the beef and stir in a drizzle of sesame oil before serving over hot rice.
